data.title: Towngas to Launch Home Safety Alert System for Elderly Residents
data.description:
Hong Kong and China Gas and the Hong Kong Federation of Trade Unions will pilot its home safety improvement program for elderly residents, the gas company said Thursday.

The Gas Guardian Care Network will monitor gas usage records and send alerts to caregivers if no gas usage is recorded from elderly residents' homes for two straight days, the report said.

data.title: B&S International Warns of Up to 50% Slump in Full-Year Profit
data.description:
B&S International expects a 40% to 50% year-on-year decline in net profit for the year ended March 31, a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The food and beverage distributor attributed the forecast to a drop in revenue and gross profit in the retail and distribution business wings amid a weakened consumer market in Hong Kong.

data.title: Escalating Middle East Tensions Weigh Down Chinese Stocks; Western Region Gold Jumps 10%
data.description:
Chinese shares tracked the losses of their global peers on Friday, as market appetite for risky assets diminished amid the escalation of conflict in the Middle East.

The Shanghai Composite Index, the main gauge of Chinese stocks, fell 0.8% to 3,377.00. The Shenzhen Component Index dropped 1.1% to 10,122.11.

Israeli airstrikes in Iran earlier on Friday resulted in the death of the chief of the Iranian Armed Forces and the commander-in-chief of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps as well as two of the country's top nuclear scientists.

On the domestic front, the Chinese government is looking to boost its cooperation with the European Central Bank, including the reform of the international monetary system.

China will also step up reviews of export license applications for rare-earth-related items, according to the Ministry of Commerce. Spokesperson He Yadong said that while some licenses have already been approved, controls remain vital due to the dual-use nature of rare earths in both military and civilian sectors.

In company news, Western Region Gold plans to acquire Xinjiang Meisheng Mining for 1.66 billion yuan. Shares of the gold smelter jumped 10% Friday.

data.title: Swiss Franc Rises as Geopolitical Tensions Boost Safe Havens — Market Talk
data.description: The Swiss franc rises after Israel launched an attack on Iran's nuclear facilities. The rising geopolitical tensions fuel demand for safe-haven assets including the franc. "The situation between Iran and Israel is likely to dominate price action on Friday," XTB's Kathleen Brooks says in a note. Any escalation could be met by a surge in safe havens and commodities, she says. The euro falls 0.2% to 0.9368 Swiss francs, having reached a three-week low of 0.9305 francs overnight, according to LSEG. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Energy Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy markets. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0730 GMT - The Swiss franc rises after Israel launched an attack on Iran's nuclear facilities. The rising geopolitical tensions fuel demand for safe-haven assets including the franc. "The situation between Iran and Israel is likely to dominate price action on Friday," XTB's Kathleen Brooks says in a note. Any escalation could be met by a surge in safe havens and commodities, she says. The euro falls 0.2% to 0.9368 Swiss francs, having reached a three-week low of 0.9305 francs overnight, according to LSEG. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0702 GMT - Oil stocks are mostly trading lower in India, after Israel launched an attack on Iran's nuclear facilities and declared a state of emergency. Shares of Indian Oil fell 0.6% and Chennai Petroleum dropped 2.2%. Hindustan Petroleum and Bharat Petroleum were down 1.6% each. Some oil stocks were higher, with Oil & Natural Gas up 2.0% and Oil India up 3.2%. The benchmark Sensex was down 0.7%. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0649 GMT - Israel's major attack on Iran's nuclear program and military leadership escalates tensions in the Middle East and triggers risk-off sentiment, creating an opportunity for those looking to sell stocks and take profits, CMC Markets' Jochen Stanzl says in a market comment. Oil prices are skyrocketing and the transportation of oil through the Strait of Hormuz is now at risk, the analyst says. The rapid oil-price surge could weigh on the euro, he says. "Investors are now grappling with the prospect of two wars and an ongoing trade conflict, prompting a reassessment of risks," Stanzl says. "Gold prices are heading towards record highs, equities are under pressure, and the dollar is rising once again. The events of the past few hours have sparked a broad risk-off movement among investors." (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0647 GMT - The dollar recovers slightly after hitting three-year lows on Thursday. Investors seek safe-haven assets after Israel launched a wide-ranging attack on Iran's nuclear facilities, Pepperstone strategist Michael Brown says in a note. Despite some views that the dollar is no longer acting as a safe haven, "when push comes to shove there is still little else that market participants want to own," he says. The DXY dollar index rises 0.3% to 98.227, having reached a low of 97.602 on Thursday when softer-than-expected U.S. producer prices data boosted bets for interest-rate cuts by the Federal Reserve. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0630 GMT - Venture Corp.'s stock looks attractively priced at one standard deviation below its mean historical price-to-earnings, RHB Research's Alfie Yeo says in a research report. The stock price's downside should also be priced in amid current dynamic tariff situation, the analyst says. Any reduction of tariffs for countries, such as Malaysia, could act as share-price catalysts for the electronics manufacturing services provider. RHB maintains a buy rating on the stock, while raising the target price to S$12.60 from S$12.50, reflecting a roll-over of its target price-to-earnings to a blended 15x 2025-2026 earnings assumption. Shares are 0.2% lower at S$11.43. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0619 GMT - Oil and defense stocks will likely be buoyed by rising tensions following Israel's major attack on Iran's nuclear and military facilities. The rest of the market should remain under pressure on Friday, Swissquote's Ipek Ozkardeskaya writes in a market comment. "Many investors may prefer to take risk off the table ahead of what could be a volatile weekend in terms of geopolitical headlines," the analyst says, adding that the news isn't entirely surprising given recent reports. Judging by the surge in oil prices, the market's response to the attack has been very strong, she notes. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0610 GMT - With Middle East tensions ignited by Israel's attack on Iran, all eyes are on where oil prices will go next. A de-escalation could bring oil back below $70 a barrel--around the 200-day moving average--returning the market's attention to supply-demand dynamics, trade disruptions and renewed pressure on Russian oil, says Ipek Ozkardeskaya, senior analyst at Swissquote Bank. A broader escalation could push oil toward $90-$100 a barrel. So far, there has been no major price action on the LNG front, but if tensions disrupt transit through the Strait of Hormuz, LNG flows could also be affected, she writes. Roughly one-fifth of global LNG passes through the waterway. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

0513 GMT - Israel's strike on Iran has put geopolitical worries back on the agenda, sparking sizeable risk-off moves, but that doesn't mean market participants should tear up long-term views, says Pepperstone's Michael Brown. While the situation remains highly fluid, he cautions that this market reaction has played out numerous times before. "Financial markets are always incredibly quick to price in geopolitical fear, but tend to be equally quick to discount it again." The bull case for equities remains intact, the senior research strategist reckons. The current weakness offers yet another dip-buying opportunity. Brown continues to be a fan of gold, which keeps proving it deserves a place in portfolios as a hedge against uncertainty, adding: "I'd not be touching crude with a bargepole for the time being." (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

0500 GMT - Chinese energy shares are broadly higher at midday after Israel's strike on Iran despite stock indexes' declines. The gains in energy stocks come as intensified tensions raise concerns about oil-supply disruptions in the Middle East, sending crude prices sharply higher. Chinese state-owned oil giant PetroChina rises 1.9% in Hong Kong and 1.1% in Shanghai. Chinese refiner Sinopec is 1.0% higher in both markets. Cnooc, another energy major, advances 2.6% in Shanghai and 2.2% in Hong Kong. China Shenhua Energy climbs 1.9% and 0.8% in Hong Kong and Shanghai, respectively. By contrast, the benchmark Shanghai Composite Index is down 0.7%. The Hang Seng Index also 0.7% lower. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Renold Agrees GBP187 Million Takeover Bid by MPE Partners
data.description:
Renold said Friday it agreed to a recommended cash takeover offer from MPE Bid Co, a new company indirectly controlled by funds managed by private equity firm MPE Mgt. Co, d/b/a MPE Partners.

Shareholders will receive 0.82 pounds sterling in cash for each share held, valuing the British industrial chains and power transmission products company's entire share capital at 186.7 million pounds sterling.

The acquisition, which only covers Renold's common shares and does not extend to the preference shares, is expected to become effective during the fourth quarter of 2025.

data.title: The CAC 40 Index Opens 1.25% Lower
data.description:
In Paris, the CAC 40 Index is falling 97 points.

Losses were driven by Accor (-3.89%), Stellantis NV (-2.72%) and Teleperformance SE (-2.33%).

data.title: Primary Health Properties Amends Assura Takeover Bid
data.description:
Primary Health Properties (, PHP.JO) on Friday reiterated its offer to acquire Assura , revising the bid with a proposed special dividend and lowering the acceptance condition.

The healthcare property group confirmed it will permit Assura to accelerate its October quarterly dividend of up to 0.0084 pound sterling per share if the offer becomes unconditional. The company also lowered the acceptance threshold to match a competing bid from Sana Bidco at 50%.

Responding to Assura's recent rejection, PHP expressed confidence in its delveraging and joint venture disposal plans. The company is also in talks with the UK Competition and Markets Authority for regulatory clearance and urged Assura to cooperate.

data.title: Stocks in Europe Down Nearly 1%
data.description:
Major European bourses were in the red on Friday, with the STOXX 50 dropping 1.4% and the STOXX 600 declining 1%, as rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East triggered a broad flight to safety.

Israel’s launched a wave of strikes against Iran targetting nuclear infrastructure and military facilities, killing two top Iranian commanders.

Israel has warned of further action, while Iran has vowed retaliation and has already deployed drones toward Israeli territory.

Travel and leisure stocks were among the weakest performers while oil, gas, and defense stocks outperformed amid heightened security risks and rising energy prices.

Deutsche Bank (-2.6%), Unicredit (-2.4%), and Adidas (-2.4%) were among the biggest laggards while stocks of Shell, Eni, Repsol, RWE, Rheinmetall, Thales, were up more than 1%.

For the week, the STOXX 50 is down 1.3%, and the STOXX 600 has lost 0.7% so far

data.title: India's IndiGo promoter likely to sell part of stake via block deals, CNBC-TV18 says
data.description:
Indian airline IndiGo's promoter group, Interglobe Enterprises, will likely sell about a 4% stake through block deals and is looking to raise around $1 billion from the sale, news channel CNBC-TV18 reported on Friday, citing sources.

Interglobe Enterprises holds a 35.71% stake in IndiGo, as per data from exchanges.

Reuters was not able to confirm the report independently. IndiGo and Interglobe Enterprises did not immediately respond to Reuters' requests for comment.

IndiGo's co-founder Rakesh Gangwal sold a 5.7% stake worth $1.36 billion in the low-cost carrier through a block deal, Reuters reported on May 27.

Shares of the airline were down all through Friday, a day after more than 240 people were killed when an Air India plane bound for London crashed moments after take-off in India's Ahmedabad city.

($1 = 86.0990 Indian rupees)

data.title: The DAX Index Opens 1.31% Lower
data.description:
In Frankfurt, the DAX Index is losing 311 points.

Losses were led by Adidas (-2.53%), Deutsche Bank (-2.52%) and Porsche AG (-2.50%).

data.title: Korea Computer Terminal Inc Wins 2.5 Billion Won Order
data.description: • KOREA COMPUTER TERMINAL INC: WINS 2.5 BILLION WON ORDER

data.title: Union Steel Trims Stake in Eneco Energy; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
Union Steel Holdings reduced its stake in Eneco Energy to 24% by selling 85 million shares to investors, Eric Low and Tank Hock Guan, for about SG$978,000, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Friday.

The company plans to use the proceeds from the sale for additional resources to explore other business opportunities.

Shares of the company were down over 3% in recent trading.

data.title: Lufthansa Extends Flight Suspension to Israel Until June 22
data.description:
Deutsche Lufthansa's flight operations to and from Tel Aviv, Israel, will remain suspended until June 22 due to the ongoing situation in the region.

The German flag carrier said Friday that affected passengers will be contacted and rebooked on alternate flights, depending on availability.

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0724 GMT - European defense stocks trade higher after Israel's major attack on Iran's nuclear program and military leadership escalated tensions in the Middle East. Britain's BAE Systems rose 3.5% shortly after the opening bell, while Germany's Rheinmetall, Hensoldt and Renk Group all gain 2% or more. Italy's Leonardo rises 2.1%, Sweden's Saab trades 2% higher and France's Thales and Dassault Aviation are up 1.2% and 1.6%, respectively. Defense and energy stocks top the Stoxx Europe 600 gainers. "Oil and defense stocks will likely benefit from rising tensions, but the rest of the market should remain under pressure," Swissquote's Ipek Ozkardeskaya says in a market comment. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0719 GMT - China's housing market may have more opportunities for higher-end properties, HSBC analysts say in commentary. The supply split for new homes in 27 key cities in June was 66% for high-end and 34% for mass market, the analysts say, citing data from real-estate services firm CRIC. It could signal the start of a significant shift to quality and away from mass-market projects that have long dominated the supply of new apartments, HSBC says. In turn, it would underpin HSBC's price-growth thesis for quality new homes, it says. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0718 GMT - Malaysia's technology sector remains clouded by tariff threats amid continuous global consumer electronics demand uncertainties and cautious investment sentiment, says Apex Securities analyst Jayden Tan. Many technology companies have cited a cautious outlook in their recent quarterly results. These companies continue to adopt a cautious stance, deferring major investments amid lack of clarity in trade policy, Tan says. However, despite near-term macro and policy uncertainties, Apex Securities maintains an overweight rating on the Malaysian technology sector. The sector has largely priced in prevailing risks, including tariff concerns and forex volatility, Tan says.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0706 GMT - SIA Engineering stands to benefit from its new contract with Singapore Airlines and Scoot, DBS Group Research's Jason Sum says in a research report. The maintenance, repair and overhaul services provider renewed its comprehensive service agreement with SIA and Scoot last month, the analyst notes. DBS estimates the contract brings high single-digit boost in MRO rates charged to SIA Group versus its prior expectation of a mid-single-digit increase. DBS lifts its FY 2026-2027 earnings estimates for SIA Engineering by 5%-6%. It also raises the stock's target price to S$3.50 from S$2.80 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 1.0% lower at S$3.04. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0702 GMT - Oil stocks are mostly trading lower in India, after Israel launched an attack on Iran's nuclear facilities and declared a state of emergency. Shares of Indian Oil fell 0.6% and Chennai Petroleum dropped 2.2%. Hindustan Petroleum and Bharat Petroleum were down 1.6% each. Some oil stocks were higher, with Oil & Natural Gas up 2.0% and Oil India up 3.2%. The benchmark Sensex was down 0.7%. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0656 GMT - European stocks are expected to open lower after Israel launched a wide-ranging attack on Iran's nuclear facilities. "The geopolitical shock came atop an already strained market landscape marked by President Trump's erratic trade policies and faltering U.S.-Iran nuclear talks," IG analysts say in a note. Risky assets take a hit while oil prices jump. Germany's DAX index is expected to open around 1.6% lower, according to IG. The U.K.'s FTSE 100 index is seen down 0.5%, with losses likely limited by gains for heavyweight oil stocks. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

0655 GMT - Israel's attack on nuclear and military targets in Iran has affected oil prices, but its effect on other commodities and markets is so far more limited, Vontobel's Manuel Lang says in a note. "This suggests a wait-and-see approach [regarding] scale, speed, and duration of Iran's retaliation, and whether its attacks will involve U.S. targets," the analyst says. He foresees markets moving to a risk-off mode. Among Swiss stocks, the luxury and travel-related names are likely to be harder hit than others. Transport and logistics disruptions could delay the flow of goods and result in a short-term increase in logistics costs. Defensive stocks and companies with solid balance sheets will weather the storm relatively well, Lang says. Some could even benefit, such as Accelleron due to shipping rerouting, he says. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0649 GMT - Israel's major attack on Iran's nuclear program and military leadership escalates tensions in the Middle East and triggers risk-off sentiment, creating an opportunity for those looking to sell stocks and take profits, CMC Markets' Jochen Stanzl says in a market comment. Oil prices are skyrocketing and the transportation of oil through the Strait of Hormuz is now at risk, the analyst says. The rapid oil-price surge could weigh on the euro, he says. "Investors are now grappling with the prospect of two wars and an ongoing trade conflict, prompting a reassessment of risks," Stanzl says. "Gold prices are heading towards record highs, equities are under pressure, and the dollar is rising once again. The events of the past few hours have sparked a broad risk-off movement among investors." (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0634 GMT - Nordic markets are expected to open sharply lower, with IG calling the OMXS30 down 1.5% at around 2444. Israel has carried out airstrikes aimed at Iran's nuclear facilities and commanders, sending oil prices soaring and hitting risk sentiment, SEB analysts say in a note. Iranian state media reported that the defense chief of the national guard has been killed, and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has said the attacks will continue until the threat is eliminated. In the financial markets, the reaction has been greatest in oil prices, where Brent crude rose by 13% overnight. Asian stock markets have fallen, while safe-haven currencies the Swiss franc, Japanese yen and U.S. dollar all strengthened. Stock-market futures indicate negative openings in both the U.S. and Europe. OMXS30 closed at 2481.49, OMXN40 at 2459.64 and OBX at 1534.32. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0630 GMT - Venture Corp.'s stock looks attractively priced at one standard deviation below its mean historical price-to-earnings, RHB Research's Alfie Yeo says in a research report. The stock price's downside should also be priced in amid current dynamic tariff situation, the analyst says. Any reduction of tariffs for countries, such as Malaysia, could act as share-price catalysts for the electronics manufacturing services provider. RHB maintains a buy rating on the stock, while raising the target price to S$12.60 from S$12.50, reflecting a roll-over of its target price-to-earnings to a blended 15x 2025-2026 earnings assumption. Shares are 0.2% lower at S$11.43. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0626 GMT - European tariffs on U.S. goods would make aircraft imports much more expensive and drive the continent's airlines to source from within the EU, according to Commerzbank analysis of the likely targets of eventual trade duties. The EU and the U.S. are for now in a truce period while they attempt to reach a deal that would avoid or minimize tariffs on each other's goods. But if President Trump's tariffs were imposed on EU goods, the bloc's retaliatory measures would hit various sectors of U.S. imports, high among them aerospace and capital goods."Aircraft are likely to become significantly more expensive in Europe as a result of the countervailing duty," the bank's analysts Vincent Stamer writes. "This increases the incentive for European airlines to switch to aircraft from inside the EU." (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby))

0619 GMT - Oil and defense stocks will likely be buoyed by rising tensions following Israel's major attack on Iran's nuclear and military facilities. The rest of the market should remain under pressure on Friday, Swissquote's Ipek Ozkardeskaya writes in a market comment. "Many investors may prefer to take risk off the table ahead of what could be a volatile weekend in terms of geopolitical headlines," the analyst says, adding that the news isn't entirely surprising given recent reports. Judging by the surge in oil prices, the market's response to the attack has been very strong, she notes. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.description: European defense stocks trade higher after Israel's major attack on Iran's nuclear program and military leadership escalated tensions in the Middle East. Britain's BAE Systems rose 3.5% shortly after the opening bell, while Germany's Rheinmetall, Hensoldt and Renk Group all gain 2% or more. Italy's Leonardo rises 2.1%, Sweden's Saab trades 2% higher and France's Thales and Dassault Aviation are up 1.2% and 1.6%, respectively. Defense and energy stocks top the Stoxx Europe 600 gainers. "Oil and defense stocks will likely benefit from rising tensions, but the rest of the market should remain under pressure," Swissquote's Ipek Ozkardeskaya says in a market comment. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

data.title: Hungary Industrial Output Contraction Confirmed at 5.0%
data.description:
Industrial production in Hungary fell by 5.0% year-on-year in April 2025, following a flat reading in the previous month, confirming initial estimates and aligning with market expectations.

The downturn was driven by a decline in output for manufacturing (-5.2% vs 0.2% in March), particularly in vehicle manufacturing, and a continued drop for mining and quarrying (-15.5% vs -10.8%).

Meanwhile, production rose for electricity, gas, steam and air-conditioning supply (6.3% vs 5%).

On a seasonally adjusted monthly basis, industrial activity rose by 1.5% in April from a 0.1% increase in the preceding period.

data.title: EPS Creative Health Technology to Turn to Full-Year Profit
data.description:
EPS Creative Health Technology Group expects a profit of HK$30 million for the year ended March 31, compared with a loss of HK$26.5 million a year prior, a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The anticipated turnaround to profit was mainly due to a gain on the disposal of EPS Innovative Medicine (Hong Kong) completed in March.

data.title: French Lender Groupe BPCE to Buy Portugal's Novo Banco for $7.4 Billion
data.description:
(07:05 GMT) *Groupe BPCE Agrees To Bid For Novo Banco
(07:06 GMT) *Groupe BPCE Signed MOU to Buy Lone Star's 75% Stake in Novo Banco
(07:07 GMT) *Groupe BPCE: Deal Values Novo Banco at Around EUR6.4B
(07:08 GMT) *Groupe BPCE in Talks with Portuguese Gov't To Buy Remaining Stake on Same Terms
(07:08 GMT) *Groupe BPCE: Transaction Expected to Close in 1H 2026
(07:25 GMT) French Lender Groupe BPCE to Buy Portugal's Novo Banco for $7.4 Billion

By Elena Vardon

Groupe BPCE agreed to buy Portuguese lender Novo Banco from its majority owner Lone Star for 6.4 billion euros ($7.41 billion) in the latest cross-border deal in European banking.

The French retail bank on Friday said it signed a memorandum of understanding to buy the 75% stake held in the group by Dallas-based private equity group Lone Star in cash. Groupe BPCE is also in talks with the Portuguese government, which owns the remaining 25% of the lender, for a deal on the same terms.

Novo Banco was created in 2014 after the multi-billion euro state bailout and breakup of Portuguese lender Banco Espirito Santo, once the country's largest bank. Since then, it has cleaned up its balance sheet and improved the profitability of its operations in a turnaround effort that included rebuilding its retail activities and corporate bank by selling assets and reducing its portfolio of nonperforming loans.

This put Novo Banco back among the country's top five lenders. Along with Banco Comercial Portugues, state-owned Caixa Geral de Depositos, Santander Portugal and CaixaBank's BPI control, they make up just under three-quarters of the country's banking assets.

The project is set to be completed in the first half of 2026.

Write to Elena Vardon at elena.vardon@wsj.com

data.title: Sterling drops as Israel's strikes on Iran boost safe-haven dollar
data.description:
The pound fell on Friday, in sync with other volatile currencies such as the Australian dollar, after Israel launched a flurry of strikes on Iran, sending investors scurrying into the relative safety of the dollar.

Sterling fell almost 0.7% to a low of $1.35225, mirroring the 0.9% declines in the Aussie and New Zealand dollars . The euro meanwhile rose 0.2% against the pound to 85.23 pence.

Israel's strikes targeted nuclear facilities, missile factories and military commanders, and Iranian media and witnesses reported explosions including at the country's main uranium enrichment facility.

Iran launched about 100 drones towards Israeli territory in retaliation, which Israel is working to intercept, Israeli military spokesman Brigadier General Effie Defrin said.

Iran has long maintained that its nuclear-related activity is for peaceful purposes.

British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said the strikes were concerning and all parties needed to step back and reduce tensions.

"Until the danger of further escalation has passed, safe assets are likely to remain in demand," Commerzbank currency strategist Michael Pfister said.

Investors were already on edge this week on lingering concern about the resilience of a U.S.-China trade truce and about the impact of existing tariffs, even those that have been rolled back from April 2's sky-high levels, on the wider economy.

The pound has faced a raft of weak UK data on manufacturing activity, employment and economic growth this week. In addition, finance minister Rachel Reeves announced her spending review, which analysts said did little to improve the outlook for growth but raised the chances of possible tax hikes later this year.

Traders expect the Bank of England to deliver another quarter-point rate cut in September and another cut at some point by December, which would bring UK interest rates down to around 3.7%, from 4.25% now (0#GBPIRPR).

data.title: Darden Restaurants, EOG Resources, Oracle
data.description:
Wall Street securities analysts revised their ratings and price targets on several U.S.-listed companies, including Darden Restaurants, EOG Resources and Oracle on Friday.

HIGHLIGHTS

  • * Darden Restaurants Inc : Jefferies raises to hold from underperform

  • * EOG Resources Inc : Jefferies raises target price to $148 from $144

  • * Oracle Corp : Stephens raises target price to $208 from $167

Following is a summary of research actions on U.S. companies reported by Reuters on Friday. Stock entries are in alphabetical order.

data.title: Berenberg Lifts Wise's Price Target on Primary US Listing Plans
data.description:
Berenberg raised Wise's price target on plans to transfer its primary listing to a US stock exchange while maintaining a secondary listing in London.

On Thursday, the research firm increased the price target to 12.70 pounds sterling from 12.40 pounds and reiterated the payment platform's buy rating. "While this change may create some short-term pressure on the shares, in the longer term we think that it will be a positive move for Wise - especially if it is included in a US index," analysts said, noting Wise reaffirmed its commitment to investing in the UK.

Analysts expect the move to also give Wise access to a larger pool of investors, greater liquidity, enhanced brand awareness and accelerated growth in the US.

Wise announced its dual-listing plans alongside strong fiscal 2025 results on June 5, with Berenberg noting that reported and cash profits surpassed consensus estimates. The company also reiterated its fiscal 2026 and medium-term guidance.

data.title: Tariff Threats Cloud Malaysia's Tech Sector — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's technology sector remains clouded by tariff threats amid continuous global consumer electronics demand uncertainties and cautious investment sentiment, says Apex Securities analyst Jayden Tan. Many technology companies have cited a cautious outlook in their recent quarterly results. These companies continue to adopt a cautious stance, deferring major investments amid lack of clarity in trade policy, Tan says. However, despite near-term macro and policy uncertainties, Apex Securities maintains an overweight rating on the Malaysian technology sector. The sector has largely priced in prevailing risks, including tariff concerns and forex volatility, Tan says.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: REG - VinaCapital Vietnam - Daily Net Asset Value
data.description:
RNS Number : 7646M VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fd. 13 June 2025  

VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund Limited

(the "Company" or "VOF")

Estimated Daily Net Asset Value ("NAV") - 12 June 2025

The Company announces that, as at the close of business on 12 June 2025, its unaudited, estimated NAV was USD 962 million or USD 7.08 per share. The Sterling equivalent as at that same date was GBP 707 million or GBP 5.21 per share.

The GBP to USD foreign exchange rate that has been applied to this NAV is 1.3592.

Disclaimers: The estimated daily NAV stated in this announcement has been prepared by the Administrator and reviewed by the Investment Manager, and is based on estimated valuations and, accordingly, the Company accepts no responsibility for the accuracy of the estimated daily NAV. The actual month end NAV in any month may vary from the estimated daily NAVs provided in the relevant month. Accordingly, no reliance should be placed on the estimated daily NAV and it has been provided for indicative purposes only. The Company's unaudited NAV as at the end of each month is published on or around the 10th of each month.

The estimated daily NAV is prepared based on the following valuation guidelines:

•

Listed equities are marked-to-market, based on the closing price of the most recent trading day, provided by the Vietnam stock exchanges (the Ho Chi Minh Stock Exchange and the Hanoi Stock Exchange).

•

Unlisted securities include over-the-counter (OTC) traded securities and unlisted securities publicly traded on the Unlisted Public Companies Market (UPCOM) of the Hanoi Stock Exchange. These holdings are marked-to-market on the same basis as listed equities. Where this valuation approach is not available, unlisted securities valuations are based on the average of up to three independent broker quotes for the last trading day of the prior week, or where these are unavailable, the last available price.

•

Non-publicly traded investments, including private equity and direct real estate investments are valued based on the last available valuation as approved by the Audit Committee, which may be adjusted for any impairment, loss or disposal.

•

The valuation of cash positions is based on cash in the Company's bank accounts at the close of the most recent trading day and is adjusted for receivables and accruals.

•

The Vietnam Dong (VND) foreign exchange rate is the daily reference rate provided by Vietcombank as of the date of the daily NAV. The British Sterling (GBP) and US Dollar (USD) exchange rates are based on the Bloomberg reference rate of the same day.

Further information is available on the Company's website at: https://vof.vinacapital.com/

Enquiries:

  • Joel Weiden

  • Investment Manager - Investor Relations and Communications

    VinaCapital Investment Management Limited

  • T: +84 28 3821 9930

  • E: joel.weiden@vinacapital.com

  • Magdala Mullegadoo

  • Company Secretary / Administrator

    Aztec Financial Services (Guernsey) Limited

  • T: +44 1481 748 814

  • E: vinacapital@aztecgroup.co.uk

1.     References to VOF or the Company in this announcement shall mean VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund Limited, a non-cellular company incorporated in the Bailiwick of Guernsey under The Companies (Guernsey) Law, 2008, with registered number 61765. It is authorised by the Guernsey Financial Services Commission (reference number 2268242) as a registered closed-ended investment scheme under The Protection of Investors (Bailiwick of Guernsey) Law, 2020 and in compliance with the Registered Collective Investment Scheme Rules, as amended.

2.     The registered office address of the Company is East Wing, Trafalgar Court, Les Banques, St Peter Port, Guernsey, Channel Islands, GY1 3PP.

3.     This announcement contains information that is inside information for the purposes of the UK version of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No. 596/2014 which is part of UK law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 (as amended and supplemented from time to time).

data.title: REG - Trainline PLC - Transaction in Own Shares
data.description:
RNS Number : 7644M Trainline PLC 13 June 2025  

TRAINLINE PLC

TRANSACTIONS IN OWN SECURITIES

Trainline plc ("Trainline") announces today that it has purchased the following number of its ordinary shares of 1 pence each on the London Stock Exchange and on Multilateral Trading Facilities from Numis Securities Limited ("Deutsche Numis"). Such purchase was affected pursuant to the share purchase programme announced by Trainline on 13 March 2025 and in accordance with the authority granted by shareholders at the 2024 Annual General Meeting of Trainline:

Ordinary Shares

Date of purchase:

12 June 2025

Number of ordinary shares purchased:

170,000

Highest price paid per share:

275.80p

Lowest price paid per share:             

271.60p

Volume weighted average price paid per share:

273.42p

The purchased shares will be cancelled.

Following the purchase of these shares, the remaining number of ordinary shares in issue will be 427,263,632. Trainline does not hold any ordinary shares in Treasury. The figure of 427,263,632 may be used by shareholders (and others with notification obligations) as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Disclosure and Transparency Rules.

In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 as it applies in the UK (the Market Abuse Regulation), a full breakdown of the individual trades made by Numis Securities Limited ("Deutsche Numis") acting as riskless principal for Trainline as part of the share buyback programme is set out in the Schedule to this announcement available through the link below:

http://www.rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com/rns/7644M_1-2025-6-13.pdf

This announcement does not constitute, or form part of, an offer or any solicitation of an offer for securities in any jurisdiction.

Enquiries

For investor enquiries, Andrew Gillian          investors@trainline.com

For media enquiries, Hollie Conway              press@trainline.com            

Brunswick Group

Simone Selzer                                                  trainline@brunswickgroup.com / +44 207 404 5959

About Trainline 

Trainline (www.trainline.com) is the leading independent rail and coach travel platform selling rail and coach tickets to millions of travellers worldwide, enabling them to seamlessly search, book and manage their journeys all in one place via its highly rated website and mobile app. Trainline is a one‐stop shop for rail and coach travel bringing together millions of routes, fares and journey times from rail and coach carriers across Europe.
